title: Eriosema arenicola
taxonomy: Eriosema arenicola is a species of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. It belongs to the genus Eriosema, which consists of about 100 species of plants. The genus Eriosema is a part of the subfamily Faboideae, which is a large and diverse group of legumes. The subfamily Faboideae is further classified under the family Fabaceae, which is a large and widespread family of flowering plants. The classification of Eriosema arenicola is a key aspect of understanding its evolutionary history and relationships with other plants.

intro: Eriosema arenicola, a shrub-like plant native to tropical Africa, belongs to the Fabaceae family and is also known as the "Desert Pea". This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. Bioactive constituents of Eriosema arenicola include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
